nodes.context: Colombian drug trafficking, neo-paramilitary organization engaged in the Colombian armed conflict.
Colombian paramilitary coalition that demobilized in 2006.
Colombian neo-paramilitary group formed by former AUC members after demobilization.
Colombian guerrilla group, enemy of Bloque Meta.
Colombian drug trafficking group, rival of Bloque Meta.
Reunited group formed from Bloque Meta and Libertadores del Vichada under leader Puntilla.

edges.evidence_quote: Bloque Meta's history can be traced back to the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, or AUC.
edges.context: Bloque Meta originated from the AUC.

edges.evidence_quote: When the AUC demobilized in 2006, many former members of the organization formed ERPAC, which would later partly become the Bloque Meta
edges.context: Bloque Meta partly formed from ERPAC.

edges.evidence_quote: to continue the counterrevolutionary struggle against the FARC in Colombia's eastern plains.
These groups fight each other over the control of drug trafficking in the eastern Meta department.
edges.context: Bloque Meta and Libertadores del Vichada fought each other.

edges.evidence_quote: the two blocs were in the hands of the drug trafficker Mauricio Pachón Rozo, also known as "Puntilla", reunited the two groups; This time with a new name: "Los Puntilleros"
edges.context: Bloque Meta and Libertadores del Vichada were reunited into Los Puntilleros.
